Title: The editor, save, and external changes
Summary: CodeMirror 6 with GDShader syntax, dirty indicator, Ctrl+S to save, and a banner for the case where someone else (or Godot) modifies your open file.
Body:
## CodeMirror with GDShader syntax

The editor is [CodeMirror 6](https://codemirror.net/) with a hand-rolled GDShader language extension. Highlighted token classes:

- **Keywords** ‚Äî `shader_type`, `uniform`, `varying`, `const`, `if`, `else`, `for`, `void`, `return`, `discard`, `render_mode`, etc. ‚Äî amber.
- **Types** ‚Äî `bool`, `int`, `float`, `vec2..4`, `mat2..4`, `sampler2D`, etc. ‚Äî cyan.
- **Built-ins** ‚Äî `TIME`, `UV`, `COLOR`, `TEXTURE`, `texture()`, `sin()`, `mix()`, `PI`, etc. ‚Äî lime.
- **Hint annotations** ‚Äî `hint_range`, `source_color`, `hint_color` ‚Äî fuchsia.
- **Numbers / strings / comments** ‚Äî emerald / emerald / muted neutral.

Indentation is 4 spaces (Godot's convention). Bracket matching is on. The theme resolves through the Bleepforge global theme variables, so it retints when you change color theme.

## Gutter diagnostics

The editor's left gutter shows diagnostic markers from two sources ‚Äî translator-level rejections (banned features, unsupported `shader_type`, reserved-name collisions) and WebGL compile errors (undeclared identifiers, type mismatches, etc.). Hover a marker to see the message; the affected text gets a subtle squiggle underline. The same diagnostics also surface in the preview pane's red banner with full message text ‚Äî one source of truth in the edit page's state, rendered in two complementary places.

## Saving

- :kbd[Ctrl+S] (or :kbd[Cmd+S] on macOS) saves through CodeMirror's keymap.
- The **Save** button does the same; disabled when there's nothing to save.
- Status pill next to the button cycles through `Unsaved` ‚Üí `Saving‚Ä¶` ‚Üí `Saved ‚úì` (2s) ‚Üí idle.
- A small amber **‚óè** next to the filename means dirty (your editor copy diverges from disk).
- The server writes atomically (temp file + rename), so a crash mid-save can't leave half a `.gdshader`.

## The external-change banner

The watcher pushes SSE events on every `.gdshader` change anywhere in the project. The edit page reacts in three ways depending on the kind of change and whether you have unsaved local edits:

- **Local is clean + remote changed**: silent reload. Your editor refreshes to match disk. No interruption.
- **Local is dirty + remote changed**: amber banner. *"Modified externally while you had unsaved edits."* Two buttons ‚Äî **Reload from disk** (discards your local) and **Keep editing** (your next save will overwrite). Nothing happens automatically.
- **Remote was deleted** (any local state): red banner. *"This shader was deleted on disk."* The editor goes read-only; you can dismiss the banner to keep the source for copy-paste, or navigate away.

> note: this also covers your own saves ‚Äî your save fires an SSE event that comes back to your window. Because `dirty === false` immediately after the save resolves, the banner code-path treats it as the silent-reload case, fetches the (identical) on-disk source, and no-ops. Same flow as a true external change, no special case for self-saves.

## Duplicate and delete

- **Duplicate** (right-click on a card OR the button in the editor header) prompts for a new filename, then writes your *current editor contents* ‚Äî not the on-disk version ‚Äî so in-progress work duplicates as expected.
- **Delete** surfaces the live usage count in the confirmation. Removes the file plus its `.gdshader.uid` sidecar (best-effort: missing sidecar is not an error).
